member.name: T:Elastic.Transport.CloudNodePool
member.summary:
An implementation that can be seeded with a cloud id and will signal the right defaults for the client to use for Elastic Cloud to .
Read more about Elastic Cloud Id:
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/cloud/current/ec-cloud-id.html

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.CloudNodePool.#ctor(System.String,Elastic.Transport.AuthorizationHeader,Elastic.Transport.DateTimeProvider)
member.summary:
An implementation that can be seeded with a cloud id and will signal the right defaults for the client to use for Elastic Cloud to .
Read more about Elastic Cloud Id here
https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/cloud/current/ec-cloud-id.html
member.param:
The Cloud Id, this is available on your cluster's dashboard and is a string in the form of cluster_name:base_64_encoded_string
Base64 encoded string contains the following tokens in order separated by $:
* Host Name (mandatory)
* Elasticsearch UUID (mandatory)
* Kibana UUID
* APM UUID
We then use these tokens to create the URI to your Elastic Cloud cluster!
Read more here: https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/cloud/current/ec-cloud-id.html
Optionally inject an instance of used to set

member.name: T:Elastic.Transport.NodePool
member.summary:
A node pool is responsible for maintaining a read only collection of (s) under .
Unlike the name might suggest this component is not responsible for IO level pooling. For that we rely on abstracting away the connection IO pooling.
This interface signals the current connection strategy to .

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.RecyclableMemoryStreamFactory.#ctor
member.summary: MemoryStream implementation that deals with pooling and managing memory streams which use potentially large buffers.
member.remarks: This class works in tandem with the RecyclableMemoryStreamManager to supply MemoryStream objects to callers, while avoiding these specific problems: 1. LOH allocations - since all large buffers are pooled, they will never incur a Gen2 GC 2. Memory waste - A standard memory stream doubles its size when it runs out of room. This leads to continual memory growth as each stream approaches the maximum allowed size. 3. Memory copying - Each time a MemoryStream grows, all the bytes are copied into new buffers. This implementation only copies the bytes when GetBuffer is called. 4. Memory fragmentation - By using homogeneous buffer sizes, it ensures that blocks of memory can be easily reused. The stream is implemented on top of a series of uniformly-sized blocks. As the stream's length grows, additional blocks are retrieved from the memory manager. It is these blocks that are pooled, not the stream object itself. The biggest wrinkle in this implementation is when GetBuffer() is called. This requires a single contiguous buffer. If only a single block is in use, then that block is returned. If multiple blocks are in use, we retrieve a larger buffer from the memory manager. These large buffers are also pooled, split by size--they are multiples/exponentials of a chunk size (1 MB by default). Once a large buffer is assigned to the stream the blocks are NEVER again used for this stream. All operations take place on the large buffer. The large buffer can be replaced by a larger buffer from the pool as needed. All blocks and large buffers are maintained in the stream until the stream is disposed (unless AggressiveBufferReturn is enabled in the stream manager).

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.Extensions.SemVersion.CompareTo(Elastic.Transport.Extensions.SemVersion)
member.summary: Compares the current instance with another object of the same type and returns an integer that indicates whether the current instance precedes, follows, or occurs in the same position in the sort order as the other object.
member.param: An object to compare with this instance.
member.returns:
A value that indicates the relative order of the objects being compared. The return value has these meanings: Value
Meaning
Less than zero
This instance precedes in the sort order.
Zero
This instance occurs in the same position in the sort order as .
Greater than zero
This instance follows in the sort order.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.CertificateValidations.AllowAll(System.Object,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Chain,System.Net.Security.SslPolicyErrors)
member.summary: DANGEROUS, never use this in production validates ALL certificates to true.
member.returns: Always true, allowing ALL certificates

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.CertificateValidations.DenyAll(System.Object,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Chain,System.Net.Security.SslPolicyErrors)
member.summary: Always false, in effect blocking ALL certificates

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.CertificateValidations.AuthorityPartOfChain(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate,System.Boolean,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509RevocationMode)
member.summary: Helper to create a certificate validation callback based on the certificate authority certificate that we used to generate the nodes certificates with. This callback expects the CA to be part of the chain as intermediate CA.
member.param: The ca certificate used to generate the nodes certificate

Custom CA are never trusted by default unless they are in the machines trusted store, set this to true if you've added the CA to the machines trusted store. In which case UntrustedRoot should not be accepted.
By default we do not check revocation, it is however recommended to check this (either offline or online).

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.CertificateValidations.AuthorityIsRoot(System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509Certificate,System.Boolean,System.Security.Cryptography.X509Certificates.X509RevocationMode)
member.summary: Helper to create a certificate validation callback based on the certificate authority certificate that we used to generate the nodes certificates with. This callback does NOT expect the CA to be part of the chain presented by the server. Including the root certificate in the chain increases the SSL handshake size. Elasticsearch's certgen by default does not include the CA in the certificate chain.
member.param: The ca certificate used to generate the nodes certificate

Custom CA are never trusted by default unless they are in the machines trusted store, set this to true if you've added the CA to the machines trusted store. In which case UntrustedRoot should not be accepted.
By default we do not check revocation, it is however recommended to check this (either offline or online).

member.name: T:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration
member.summary: All the transport configuration that you as the user can use to steer the behavior of the and all the components such as and .

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.Authentication
member.summary: The HTTP authorization request header used to provide credentials that authenticate a user agent with a server.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.BootstrapLock
member.summary: Provides a semaphoreslim to transport implementations that need to limit access to a resource

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ClientCertificates
member.summary: Use the following certificates to authenticate all HTTP requests. You can also set them on individual request using

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.Connection
member.summary: The connection abstraction behind which all actual IO happens

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ConnectionLimit
member.summary:
Limits the number of concurrent connections that can be opened to an endpoint. Defaults to 80 (see ).
For Desktop CLR, this setting applies to the DefaultConnectionLimit property on the ServicePointManager object when creating ServicePoint objects, affecting the default implementation.
For Core CLR, this setting applies to the MaxConnectionsPerServer property on the HttpClientHandler instances used by the HttpClient inside the default implementation

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.NodePool
member.summary: The connection pool to use when talking with Elasticsearch

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ProductRegistration
member.summary: Returns information about the current product making use of the transport.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.DeadTimeout
member.summary: The time to put dead nodes out of rotation (this will be multiplied by the number of times they've been dead)

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.DisableAutomaticProxyDetection
member.summary: Disabled proxy detection on the webrequest, in some cases this may speed up the first connection your appdomain makes, in other cases it will actually increase the time for the first connection. No silver bullet! use with care!

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.DisableDirectStreaming
member.summary: When set to true will disable (de)serializing directly to the request and response stream and return a byte[] copy of the raw request and response. Defaults to false

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.DisableAuditTrail
member.summary: When set to true will disable capturing an audit trail for requests.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.DisablePings
member.summary: This signals that we do not want to send initial pings to unknown/previously dead nodes and just send the call straightaway

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.EnableHttpCompression
member.summary: Enable gzip compressed requests and responses

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.Headers
member.summary: Try to send these headers for every request

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.HttpPipeliningEnabled
member.summary: Whether HTTP pipelining is enabled. The default is true

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.KeepAliveInterval
member.summary: KeepAliveInterval - specifies the interval, in milliseconds, between when successive keep-alive packets are sent if no acknowledgement is received.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.KeepAliveTime
member.summary: KeepAliveTime - specifies the timeout, in milliseconds, with no activity until the first keep-alive packet is sent.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.MaxDeadTimeout
member.summary: The maximum amount of time a node is allowed to marked dead

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.MaxRetries
member.summary: When a retryable exception occurs or status code is returned this controls the maximum amount of times we should retry the call to Elasticsearch

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.MaxRetryTimeout
member.summary:
Limits the total runtime including retries separately from
When not specified defaults to which itself defaults to 60 seconds

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.MemoryStreamFactory
member.summary: Provides a memory stream factory

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.NodePredicate
member.summary: Register a predicate to select which nodes that you want to execute API calls on. Note that sniffing requests omit this predicate and always execute on all nodes. When using an implementation that supports reseeding of nodes, this will default to omitting master only node from regular API calls. When using static or single node connection pooling it is assumed the list of node you instantiate the client with should be taken verbatim.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.OnRequestCompleted
member.summary: Allows you to register a callback every time a an API call is returned

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.OnRequestDataCreated
member.summary: An action to run when the for a request has been created.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ParseAllHeaders
member.summary: When enabled, all headers from the HTTP response will be included in the .

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.PingTimeout
member.summary: The timeout in milliseconds to use for ping requests, which are issued to determine whether a node is alive

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ProxyAddress
member.summary: When set will force all connections through this proxy

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ProxyPassword
member.summary: The password for the proxy, when configured

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ProxyUsername
member.summary: The username for the proxy, when configured

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.QueryStringParameters
member.summary: Append these query string parameters automatically to every request

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.RequestResponseSerializer
member.summary: The serializer to use to serialize requests and deserialize responses

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.RequestTimeout
member.summary: The timeout in milliseconds for each request to Elasticsearch

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ResponseHeadersToParse
member.summary: A containing the names of all HTTP response headers to attempt to parse and included on the .

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ServerCertificateValidationCallback
member.summary: Register a ServerCertificateValidationCallback per request

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.CertificateFingerprint
member.summary: During development, the server certificate fingerprint may be provided. When present, it is used to validate the certificate sent by the server. The fingerprint is expected to be the hex string representing the SHA256 public key fingerprint.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.SkipDeserializationForStatusCodes
member.summary: Configure the client to skip deserialization of certain status codes e.g: you run Elasticsearch behind a proxy that returns an unexpected json format

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.SniffInformationLifeSpan
member.summary: Force a new sniff for the cluster when the cluster state information is older than the specified timespan

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.SniffsOnConnectionFault
member.summary: Force a new sniff for the cluster state every time a connection dies

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.SniffsOnStartup
member.summary: Sniff the cluster state immediately on startup

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.ThrowExceptions
member.summary:
Instead of following a c/go like error checking on response.IsValid do throw an exception (except when is false) on the client when a call resulted in an exception on either the client or the Elasticsearch server.
Reasons for such exceptions could be search parser errors, index missing exceptions, etc...

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.UrlFormatter
member.summary: Access to instance that is aware of this instance

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.UserAgent
member.summary: The user agent string to send with requests. Useful for debugging purposes to understand client and framework versions that initiate requests to Elasticsearch

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.ITransportConfiguration.StatusCodeToResponseSuccess
member.summary:
Allow you to override the status code inspection that sets
Defaults to validating the statusCode is greater or equal to 200 and less then 300
When the request is using 404 is valid out of the box as well

summary.summary: Allows you to control how behaves and where/how it connects to Elastic Stack products

param.name: nodePool
param.summary:
A node pool is responsible for maintaining a read only collection of (s) under .
Unlike the name might suggest this component is not responsible for IO level pooling. For that we rely on abstracting away the connection IO pooling.
This interface signals the current connection strategy to .

param.name: invoker
param.summary:
This interface abstracts the actual IO performs.
holds a single instance of this class
The instance to be used is provided to the constructor of implementations
Where its exposed under

param.summary:
When the needs to (de)serialize anything it will call into the implementation of this base class.
e.g: Whenever the receives to serialize that data.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.EnableTcpKeepAlive(System.TimeSpan,System.TimeSpan)
member.summary:
Sets the keep-alive option on a TCP connection.
For Desktop CLR, sets ServicePointManager.SetTcpKeepAlive

param.name: keepAliveTime
param.summary: KeepAliveTime - specifies the timeout, in milliseconds, with no activity until the first keep-alive packet is sent.

param.name: keepAliveInterval
param.summary: KeepAliveInterval - specifies the interval, in milliseconds, between when successive keep-alive packets are sent if no acknowledgement is received.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.MaximumRetries(System.Int32)
member.summary: When a retryable exception occurs or status code is returned this controls the maximum amount of times we should retry the call to Elasticsearch

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.ConnectionLimit(System.Int32)

summary.summary:
Limits the number of concurrent connections that can be opened to an endpoint. Defaults to 80 (see ).
For Desktop CLR, this setting applies to the DefaultConnectionLimit property on the ServicePointManager object when creating ServicePoint objects, affecting the default implementation.
For Core CLR, this setting applies to the MaxConnectionsPerServer property on the HttpClientHandler instances used by the HttpClient inside the default implementation

member.param: The connection limit, a value lower then 0 will cause the connection limit not to be set at all

member.summary: Force a new sniff for the cluster state every time a connection dies

member.summary: Sniff the cluster state immediately on startup

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.SniffLifeSpan(System.Nullable{System.TimeSpan})

summary.summary: Force a new sniff for the cluster when the cluster state information is older than the specified timespan

member.param: The duration a clusterstate is considered fresh, set to null to disable periodic sniffing

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.NodePredicate(System.Func{Elastic.Transport.Node,System.Boolean})
member.summary: Register a predicate to select which nodes that you want to execute API calls on. Note that sniffing requests omit this predicate and always execute on all nodes. When using an implementation that supports reseeding of nodes, this will default to omitting master only node from regular API calls. When using static or single node connection pooling it is assumed the list of node you instantiate the client with should be taken verbatim.
member.param: Return true if you want the node to be used for API calls

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.EnableDebugMode(System.Action{Elastic.Transport.ApiCallDetails})
member.summary: Turns on settings that aid in debugging like DisableDirectStreaming() and PrettyJson() so that the original request and response JSON can be inspected. It also always asks the server for the full stack trace on errors
member.param: An optional callback to be performed when the request completes. This will not overwrite the global OnRequestCompleted callback that is set directly on ConnectionSettings. If no callback is passed, DebugInformation from the response will be written to the debug output by default.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.PrettyJson(System.Boolean)
member.summary:

member.name: T:Elastic.Transport.DynamicResponse
member.summary:
A type of response that makes it easier to work with responses in an untyped fashion.
It exposes the body as which is `dynamic` through
Since `dynamic` can be scary in .NET this response also exposes a safe traversal mechanism under which support an xpath'esque syntax to fish for values in the returned json.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.DynamicResponse.Get``1(System.String)
member.summary:
Traverses data using path notation.
e.g some.deep.nested.json.path
A special lookup is available for ANY key using _arbitrary_key_ e.g some.deep._arbitrary_key_.json.path which will traverse into the first key
member.param: path into the stored object, keys are separated with a dot and the last key is returned as T
member.returns: T or default

member.name: T:Elastic.Transport.DynamicValue
member.summary:
Represents a value returned by .
This is `dynamic` through and supports direct casting various valuetypes
Since `dynamic` can be scary in .NET this response also exposes a safe traversal mechanism under which support an xpath'esque syntax to fish for values in the returned json.

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.DynamicValue.TryBinaryOperation(System.Dynamic.BinaryOperationBinder,System.Object,System.Object@)
member.summary: Provides implementation for binary operations. Classes derived from the class can override this method to specify dynamic behavior for operations such as addition and multiplication.
member.returns: true if the operation is successful; otherwise, false. If this method returns false, the run-time binder of the language determines the behavior. (In most cases, a language-specific run-time exception is thrown.)

member.name: M:Elastic.Transport.DynamicValue.TryConvert(System.Dynamic.ConvertBinder,System.Object@)
member.summary: Provides implementation for type conversion operations. Classes derived from the class can override this method to specify dynamic behavior for operations that convert an object from one type to another.
member.returns: true if the operation is successful; otherwise, false. If this method returns false, the run-time binder of the language determines the behavior. (In most cases, a language-specific run-time exception is thrown.)
member.param: Provides information about the conversion operation. The binder.Type property provides the type to which the object must be converted. For example, for the statement (String)sampleObject in C# (CType(sampleObject, Type) in Visual Basic), where sampleObject is an instance of the class derived from the class, binder.Type returns the type. The binder.Explicit property provides information about the kind of conversion that occurs. It returns true for explicit conversion and false for implicit conversion.
The result of the type conversion operation.

Reasons for such exceptions could be search parser errors, index missing exceptions, etc...

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#ConnectionLimit
member.summary:
Limits the number of concurrent connections that can be opened to an endpoint. Defaults to 80 (see ).
For Desktop CLR, this setting applies to the DefaultConnectionLimit property on the ServicePointManager object when creating ServicePoint objects, affecting the default implementation.
For Core CLR, this setting applies to the MaxConnectionsPerServer property on the HttpClientHandler instances used by the HttpClient inside the default implementation

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#NodePool
member.summary: The connection pool to use when talking with Elasticsearch

member.summary: The time to put dead nodes out of rotation (this will be multiplied by the number of times they've been dead)

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#DisableAutomaticProxyDetection
member.summary: Disabled proxy detection on the webrequest, in some cases this may speed up the first connection your appdomain makes, in other cases it will actually increase the time for the first connection. No silver bullet! use with care!

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#DisableDirectStreaming
member.summary: When set to true will disable (de)serializing directly to the request and response stream and return a byte[] copy of the raw request and response. Defaults to false

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#DisableAuditTrail
member.summary: When set to true will disable capturing an audit trail for requests.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#DisablePings
member.summary: This signals that we do not want to send initial pings to unknown/previously dead nodes and just send the call straightaway

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#EnableHttpCompression
member.summary: Enable gzip compressed requests and responses

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#Headers
member.summary: Try to send these headers for every request

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#KeepAliveTime
member.summary: KeepAliveTime - specifies the timeout, in milliseconds, with no activity until the first keep-alive packet is sent.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#MaxDeadTimeout
member.summary: The maximum amount of time a node is allowed to marked dead

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#MaxRetryTimeout
member.summary:
Limits the total runtime including retries separately from
When not specified defaults to which itself defaults to 60 seconds

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#NodePredicate
member.summary: Register a predicate to select which nodes that you want to execute API calls on. Note that sniffing requests omit this predicate and always execute on all nodes. When using an implementation that supports reseeding of nodes, this will default to omitting master only node from regular API calls. When using static or single node connection pooling it is assumed the list of node you instantiate the client with should be taken verbatim.

member.name: P:Elastic.Transport.TransportConfigurationBase`1.Elastic#Transport#ITransportConfiguration#ThrowExceptions
member.summary:
Instead of following a c/go like error checking on response.IsValid do throw an exception (except when is false) on the client when a call resulted in an exception on either the client or the Elasticsearch server.
Reasons for such exceptions could be search parser errors, index missing exceptions, etc...
